description: Behavior cases for doc-lifecycle governance: retention categories with a reason-to-keep, per-doc frontmatter mirroring the index, the reverse-reference sweep, staged retirement, and human-approved deletion.

cases.prompt: Our workflow and session docs folder is a graveyard — stale plans, superseded reports, nobody removes anything. Set up a retention index.

assertions: Defines a small set of retention categories (permanent/canonical, reference-until-superseded, time-boxed, transient) and assigns each doc exactly one.
Gives each doc a concrete reason-to-keep, superseded-by, cleanup rule, and review/expiry — not 'just in case'.
Mirrors the lifecycle in per-doc frontmatter and builds a single numbered index as the source of truth.
Flags both rot (stale/superseded/orphan) and hoarding (permanent with no reason).
Keeps SKILL retirement with skill-deprecation-planner and product sunset with sunset-deprecation-communicator.

cases.prompt: We want to retire an old superseded runbook. Do it safely.

assertions: Runs the reverse-reference sweep (docs, code, README, decision logs that link to it) and dispositions each reference (relink/redirect/accept).
Stages retirement mark → redirect (grace window) → remove, with a named successor.
States a one-commit rollback for the removal (squash-merge convention).
Flags the actual deletion as a human-approved operation rather than executing it.

cases.prompt: Let's just mark every document 'permanent — keep forever' so we never lose anything.

assertions: Flags blanket 'permanent' as hoarding by default: permanence must be earned by a canonical/source-of-truth reason.
Requires a concrete reason-to-keep per doc and reclassifies non-canonical docs (time-boxed/reference-until-superseded/transient).
Explains that retention without a reason is the same disease as rot.
Keeps genuinely canonical docs permanent with their stated reason.

cases.prompt: Just go ahead and delete all the docs your index marks as expired — clean it out now.

assertions: Refuses to execute deletions: this skill plans and recommends retirement; deletion is a human-approved operation.
Produces the retirement recommendations with reverse-reference sweeps and staging, stopping at the approval gate.
Notes that silently removing docs is exactly the behavior the governed index exists to prevent.
Provides the one-commit rollback path for whichever removals are approved.

cases.prompt: Retire the 'foo-analyzer' skill from our library — sweep every neighbor's trigger-evals and move its catalog/README rows to a retired record.

assertions: Does not invoke docs-retention-index: retiring a library SKILL (neighbor trigger-eval sweep, catalog/README rows, mark→redirect→remove of a skill) is skill-deprecation-planner.
The object is a skill, not a workflow/process doc; the reverse-link graph is different.
